mission: Map current state of event-related files for truth closure resumption

key_types.EventName (const): lines 11-112 — `as const` object with PascalCase keys, dot-notation string values
key_types.EventNameValues: line 118 — `readonly EventName[]` array of all values

key_functions.encodeForDb: lines 34-58 — maps RuntimeEvent → DB row (snake_case columns)
key_functions.query (effect): lines 114-130 — query with filters, pagination, ordering

event-store.ts.note: No direct reference to EventName; stores whatever eventType string arrives. No toStoreEvent here — that lives in secretary.ts

critical.eventType has no schema constraint: line 12 — Schema.String allows any arbitrary string, no reference to EventName or enum

key_types.TypedToolResult<T>: lines 30-40 — tool, status, errorKind?, recoverable, summary, data, failures?, suggestedNextTools, nextAffordances

constructors.makeFailure: lines 87-106 — status: "failed", takes errorKind + recoverable + failures

classifyError.note: classifyError NEVER produces "permission_denied" — that errorKind is only set by makeDenied directly

fromDefect.dead_code: The `errorKind === "permission_denied"` branch is unreachable via classifyError. Only makeDenied produces "permission_denied" but doesn't go through fromDefect.

typed-result.ts.wrapExecute: lines 414-438 — wraps tool execute Effect: catches defects → fromDefect, success → makeSuccess + enrichExecuteResult
typed-result.ts.enrichExecuteResult: lines 386-403 — attaches typedResult, toolStatus, toolErrorKind, toolRecoverable, suggestedNextTools to ExecuteResult.metadata
typed-result.ts.formatOutput: lines 342-366 — formats [OK]/[DENIED]/[CANCELLED]/[KIND] prefixes + structured JSON suffix

key_functions.inferActor: lines 12-22 — infers from eventType prefix: session/server/instance→lifecycle, question/permission→user, tool/command/file→tool, llm/agent/session.next→assistant, else→system
key_functions.extractStatus: lines 33-49 — reads properties.phase or properties.status, case-insensitive, validates against EventStatus values (started/succeeded/failed/denied/cancelled/recovered)
key_functions.toRuntimeEvent: lines 51-79 — maps bus payload to RuntimeEvent. eventType = payload.type (bus dot-notation). status = extractStatus(props).
key_functions.layer: lines 81-103 — subscribes to bus.subscribeAll(), bridges each event to EventStore.record()

index.ts.missing: EventName constants are NOT re-exported from the event module barrel

secretary.ts (toStoreEvent).problem: eventType: event._tag — stores PascalCase tags ("LaneCreated", "Failed", "Blocked") directly as the eventType string, bypassing EventName registry entirely

fields_set.payloadJson: event (the full state machine event object)

secretary.ts (toStoreEvent).toSMEvent: lines 121-155 — maps RuntimeEvent._tag to bus event type string (dot-notation). E.g. "LaneCreated" → "lane.created", "Failed" → "permission.denied"

event_naming_vocabularies.summary: 5 distinct naming vocabularies coexist with no shared schema authority

vocabularies.name: EventName constants
vocabularies.authority: Canonical registry, but NOT enforced — nothing requires eventType values to come from here

vocabularies.name: Bus event type strings
vocabularies.format: lowercase dot-notation string literals passed as `{ type: "..." }`
vocabularies.gap: MANY types used here are NOT in EventName. Missing: plan.produced, plan.approved, plan.rejected, edit.applied, validation.completed, validation.failure, child.blocked, context.sufficient, lane.created, claim.conflict, file.conflict, user.approval, file.read, tool.failed, redteam.finding, critic.review, artifact.plan, plan.created, lane.completed, child.completed, file.conflict, user.approval.something, validation, typecheck, test, permission.denied

vocabularies.name: Secretary state machine _tag discriminator
vocabularies.gap: These get stored directly as eventType by toStoreEvent() (PascalCase in DB)

vocabularies.name: State machine `kind` field
vocabularies.format: snake_case strings used in `when: { kind: "..." }` guards
vocabularies.gap: Distinct format (snake_case) — different from both PascalCase and dot-notation

vocabularies.name: Flight recorder prefixes
vocabularies.format: Prefix strings used for allowlist matching
vocabularies.gap: References "tool.started" and "tool.completed" which exist in neither EventName nor schema-backed EventStatus

EventStatus_Schema.backing: Schema.Literals (enforced by Effect Schema at runtime)

FlightRecorderEvent_status.backing: Optional untyped string (no enforcement at all)

tool_failure_to_status_mapping.flow: error → classifyError(error) → { errorKind, recoverable, summary } → map to ToolStatus

mapping.permission_denied: NEVER produced by classifyError (dead code branch) → ToolStatus: "denied"

tool_failure_to_status_mapping.problem: classifyError cannot produce "permission_denied" — the fromDefect branch is dead code. Permission denial can only come from the separate makeDenied() constructor.
tool_failure_to_status_mapping.recovered: NEVER produced by fromDefect or any tool status constructor. Only EventStatus lists it.
tool_failure_to_status_mapping.started: NEVER produced by any ToolStatus — not part of ToolStatus type. Only in EventStatus.

gaps_and_smoking_guns.title: EventName registry not enforced as constraint
gaps_and_smoking_guns.detail: RuntimeEvent.eventType is Schema.String with no enum constraint. Any string passes. EventName exists as a separate registry but nothing references it as a validator.

gaps_and_smoking_guns.title: EventName not re-exported from index.ts
gaps_and_smoking_guns.detail: The barrel export (index.ts) does NOT export EventName, EventNameValues, or the EventName type. Consumers must import directly from ./event/event-names.

gaps_and_smoking_guns.title: toStoreEvent stores PascalCase tags as eventType
gaps_and_smoking_guns.detail: toStoreEvent() sets eventType: event._tag which passes PascalCase discriminator tags ("LaneCreated", "Failed", "Blocked") directly into the RuntimeEvent DB column. This contradicts the dot-notation convention used everywhere else.

gaps_and_smoking_guns.title: 20+ bus event types not registered in EventName
gaps_and_smoking_guns.detail: The bus event type strings used in toSMEvent, state-machine guards, and predicates include many values with no EventName entry: plan.produced, plan.approved, plan.rejected, edit.applied, validation.completed, validation.failure, child.blocked, context.sufficient, lane.created, claim.conflict, file.conflict, user.approval, file.read, tool.failed, redteam.finding, critic.review, artifact.plan, plan.created, lane.completed, child.completed, permission.denied.

gaps_and_smoking_guns.title: ToolStatus and EventStatus are desynchronized enums
gaps_and_smoking_guns.detail: ToolStatus ("succeeded" | "failed" | "denied" | "cancelled") is missing "started" and "recovered" from EventStatus. ToolStatus is a raw TS union (no runtime enforcement). EventStatus is Schema.Literals (enforced). No shared authority.

gaps_and_smoking_guns.title: fromDefect dead code: permission_denied unreachable
gaps_and_smoking_guns.detail: classifyError (line 136) produces error kinds: invalid_arguments, tool_error, fatal_tool_error, timeout, transient, validation, cancelled, unexpected. It NEVER produces "permission_denied". The `errorKind === "permission_denied"` branch in fromDefect (line 193) is dead code.

gaps_and_smoking_guns.title: toStoreEvent sets status = undefined always
gaps_and_smoking_guns.detail: The toStoreEvent function hardcodes `status: undefined` despite the campaign lifecycle events having implicit statuses (e.g., a "Failed" event should map to status: "failed").

gaps_and_smoking_guns.title: State machine uses THREE different event naming conventions simultaneously
gaps_and_smoking_guns.detail: Secretary uses PascalCase _tag + toSMEvent dot-notation. State-machine guard kinds use snake_case ("plan_produced"). Store eventType uses PascalCase _tag directly. Three conventions in one subsystem.

gaps_and_smoking_guns.title: Flight recorder references event types not in any schema
gaps_and_smoking_guns.detail: RECORDED_EVENT_PREFIXES includes "tool.started" and "tool.completed" which exist in neither EventName registry nor EventStatus. ToolStatus has "succeeded" and "failed" but not "started".

unify_taxonomies: Register missing bus event types in EventName (all ~20 missing values from predicates.ts, secretary.ts, state-machine.ts)
Consider making EventName the single authority: either (a) constrain RuntimeEvent.eventType to EventName via a Schema.transform or Schema.filter, or (b) generate EventName from a central definition
Align state-machine `kind` values with EventName dot-notation (e.g., "plan_produced" → "plan.produced") or keep them separate but document the mapping
map_tool_failures_to_status: Add "started" to ToolStatus or keep ToolStatus separate from EventStatus but make the relationship explicit
Fix fromDefect dead code: either make classifyError handle permission_denied, or remove the dead branch
Ensure toStoreEvent maps status meaningfully: extract status from the campaign event type (e.g., "Failed" → status: "failed", "Blocked" → status: "denied")
Consider deriving EventStatus from ToolStatus + extra states rather than maintaining two independent lists
Remove or standardize flight-recorder's "tool.started"/"tool.completed" against the unified vocabulary
